hawkish_phrases: Inflation remained far too high and was likely to stay above target for an extended period
inflation could rise further in the near term
price pressures had continued to strengthen and broaden across the economy
inflation had started to become self-reinforcing
the risks to the inflation outlook were primarily on the upside
dovish_phrases: the economy expected to stagnate later in 2022 and in the first quarter of 2023
risks to growth were primarily on the downside, particularly in the near term
a recession was becoming increasingly likely
there were no convincing signs that second-round effects were already materialising
summary: The Governing Council unanimously raised the three key ECB interest rates by 75 basis points in September 2022, frontloading the transition from highly accommodative policy rates with a strong hawkish bias, signaling that interest rates would be raised further over subsequent meetings to bring inflation back to 2% despite downside risks to growth.
